steps.title: Explain the captive-wild gap
steps.content: The blank must explain why captive primate studies tend to show more right-handedness than wild primate studies. The needed completion should address that broad captive-versus-wild pattern, not just one study's sample size.

steps.title: Reject narrow explanations
steps.content: Choice A questions only the wild chimpanzee sample. Choice B questions only the captive gorilla sample. Choice D compares methods in two named studies. These do not explain the broader pattern across captive and wild primate studies.

steps.title: Use human exposure
steps.content: Choice C gives a causal mechanism: captive gorillas have more exposure to right-handed humans, which could lead them to acquire more right-handed behaviors. That explains why captive groups might show higher right-handedness. The answer is C.
